intro_cards.title: Special Characters: を and ん
intro_cards.body:
Two final characters with special roles:

を is almost exclusively used as a grammar particle (marking the object of a verb). It's pronounced 'o.'

ん is the only kana that represents a single consonant — 'n.' It never starts a word.

With this lesson, you'll complete all 71 hiragana characters — 46 basic, 20 dakuten, and 5 handakuten!
